hook: Jamie arrives at an empty house on Crane Street for what seems like a simple clearing job — until he opens the cupboard beside the fireplace.
goal: Complete the clearing job Margaret hired him for; but once Jamie finds the camera and watches the footage, his real goal becomes understanding what was hidden under that floorboard — and who else already knows.

start_state.location: The Abandoned House, Crane Street (loc_001), moving into the Front Room (loc_002)
knowledge: He was hired by Margaret Voss to clear the house at Crane Street
The house has been empty for many years
He will be paid when the job is done

continuity_rules: This is Episode 1 — no prior episode events to contradict or continue
The metal box must remain under the floorboard at the end of this episode — Jamie must not lift the board
Mrs. Holt must be established as watching but must not speak to Jamie in this episode
key_events: Jamie enters the front room and begins clearing — he opens the old cupboard beside the fireplace and finds, tucked at the back behind a folded rag, a small video camera wrapped tightly in a piece of cloth.

Jamie charges the camera using his phone's power bank and watches the footage: grainy but clear enough to follow — an unknown man carefully lifts a floorboard near the bay window, places a rectangular metal box underneath, and replaces the board. The man glances toward the camera once; his face is visible, but Jamie does not recognise him.

Margaret calls to ask how the work is going and tells Jamie firmly she wants the house cleared by the end of the week. Jamie says progress is fine. He does not mention the camera — not because he has a plan, but because something stops him on impulse; the word 'fine' is out of his mouth before he has thought about it.

Jamie crosses to the floorboard shown in the footage. He crouches and sees that the thick dust around it has been wiped away and fresh scratch marks run along the board's edge — this was done recently, not decades ago. He straightens and looks toward the bay window; through the glass, Mrs. Holt is standing at the window of the house next door, watching him without moving.
major_reveal: The floorboard shown in the footage has been recently disturbed — meaning someone else already knows about the hiding spot and visited the house after the camera recorded the original hiding.
new_thread_created: Who recently disturbed the floorboard — and why did they leave without taking what was hidden beneath it?
tone_guardrails: Keep Jamie's reactions physical and understated — no dramatic internal speeches, no moment of sudden certainty
One major revelation only: the floorboard has been recently disturbed
Do not introduce the Warner family name or any backstory about the house's history
Margaret's phone call must feel routine on the surface; the tension lives only in what Jamie chooses to withhold, not in anything Margaret says directly
forbidden_moves: Do not show or name the contents of the metal box
Do not reveal who disturbed the floorboard
Do not name the Warner family
Do not let Mrs. Holt speak to Jamie or make contact in any way
Do not let Jamie lift the floorboard
Do not have Margaret appear in person — the phone call is sufficient
ending_hook: Jamie stands over the disturbed floorboard in an empty house, camera in hand, knowing someone was here before him — and that Mrs. Holt, still and silent in the window next door, has probably been watching for longer than he has.
